introSection: Twisted Timbers is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA. It opened on March 24, 2018 and is a Rocky Mountain Construction I-Box refurbishment of Hurler, which opened in 1994 and closed in 2015.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Track for the new ride was spotted at Kings Dominion in April 2017.

Details about the ride, including its name, were announced on August 16, 2017.

Twisted Timbers opened to the public on March 24, 2018, three years after the closure of Hurler.

In 2019, a metal detector was installed in the queue line and cell phone pouches were added to the trains.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Twisted Timbers has three inversions, three overbanked turns and twenty moments of airtime.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The train leaves the station by making a right turn and climbs the tall chain lift hill. At the top, riders hit a right turn and flip into a tall barrel roll downdrop. They reach a max speed of upon touching down the ground. The train rolls through a small airtime hill and a right over-banked curve. After going through three hills, riders go through a cutback. Then, the train goes through some smaller airtime hills. Riders move through a right turn and a zero-g roll. This is followed by a right turn and several airtime hills. The train then hits the final brake run and riders return to the station.

fullText:
Twisted Timbers is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA. It opened on March 24, 2018 and is a Rocky Mountain Construction I-Box refurbishment of Hurler, which opened in 1994 and closed in 2015.

Track for the new ride was spotted at Kings Dominion in April 2017.

Details about the ride, including its name, were announced on August 16, 2017.

Twisted Timbers opened to the public on March 24, 2018, three years after the closure of Hurler.

In 2019, a metal detector was installed in the queue line and cell phone pouches were added to the trains.

Twisted Timbers has three inversions, three overbanked turns and twenty moments of airtime.

The train leaves the station by making a right turn and climbs the tall chain lift hill. At the top, riders hit a right turn and flip into a tall barrel roll downdrop. They reach a max speed of upon touching down the ground. The train rolls through a small airtime hill and a right over-banked curve. After going through three hills, riders go through a cutback. Then, the train goes through some smaller airtime hills. Riders move through a right turn and a zero-g roll. This is followed by a right turn and several airtime hills. The train then hits the final brake run and riders return to the station.
